questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Barium (Ba) is an element in Group II of the Periodic Table. Its chemical properties are similar to those of calcium.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Describe the bonding in barium.
sub_questions.answer: It is the metallic bond between delocalised electrons and barium ions. 2

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: A gas with a pungent smell is formed when  \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_2(s)} \)  is heated with  \( NH_4Cl(s) \) . State the reason why the gas CANNOT be collected by each of the following methods.

children.label: i
children.question_text:
[IMG:q1_img_0.jpg]

Reason:
children.answer: Ammonia is less dense than air.

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
[IMG:q1_img_1.jpg]

Reason:
children.answer: Ammonia is soluble in water.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
An experiment was carried out to study the change in electrical conductivity of the mixture formed when a dilute  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  was added gradually to a fixed volume of a dilute  \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_{2}(aq)} \) . The graph below shows the results of the experiment.

[IMG:q1_img_2.png]

children.label: i
children.question_text: State the expected observation when dilute  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \) is added to dilute \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_{2}(aq)} \) .
children.answer: White solid forms. / White precipitate forms. / Heat evolves.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Explain the change of electrical conductivity in the following stages :
From A to B
children.answer: When  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \) is added, \( BaSO_{4}(s) \) and \( H_{2}O(l) \) are formed, the concentration of mobile ions in the mixture decreases.

children.question_text: From B to C
children.answer:
Excess  \( H^{+}(aq) \)  and  \( SO_{4}^{2-}(aq) \)  ions are introduced into the solution. / 
The concentrations of  \( H^{+}(aq) \)  and  \( SO_{4}^{2-}(a_{q}) \)  ions in the solution increase. / 
The concentration of mobile ions in the solution increases when  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  is in excess.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Water pipes used to carry drinking water are commonly made of copper instead of iron. Although lead-containing solder can be used to join these water pipes, such use is prohibited.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest one chemical property of copper that makes it more suitable than iron for making water pipes. Explain your answer.
sub_questions.answer:
• Copper is not so easily oxidised / corroded as iron. 
• Copper has a lower tendency to lose electrons than iron.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Suggest one reason of adding lead to soldering materials.
children.answer: It is to lower the melting point of soldering materials.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Explain why lead-containing solder is prohibited in joining these water pipes.
children.answer: Lead is / Compounds of lead are toxic / poisonous.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
A city stipulates that the concentration of lead ions in drinking water should not exceed  \( 1.0 \times 10^{-8} \)  g cm\( ^{-3} \) . Express this concentration in mol dm\( ^{-3} \) .
(Relative atomic mass: Pb = 207.2)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why propene can form a polymer, but propane cannot.
sub_questions.answer: A propene molecule has C=C bond whereas propane molecule has not.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why  \( \mathrm{HO_{2}C(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H} \)  can form a polymer with  \( \mathrm{H_{2}N(CH_{2})_{6}NH_{2}} \) , but  \( \mathrm{CH_{3}(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H} \)  cannot.
sub_questions.answer:
•  \( HO_{2}C(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H \)  has two  \( -CO_{2}H \)  groups to react with  \( -NH_{2} \)  group but  \( CH_{3}(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H \)  has only one  \( -CO_{2}H \)  group. 
• Each  \( HO_{2}C(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H \)  molecule can react with two  \( H_{2}N(CH_{2})_{6}NH_{2} \)  molecules to form a chain but  \( CH_{3}(CH_{2})_{4}CO_{2}H \) cannot.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Describe the formation of dative covalent bond using  \( H_{3}O^{+} \)  as example.
sub_questions.answer:
• The O atom in  \( H_{2}O \)  has lone pairs of electrons. 1
•  \( H^{+} \)  does not have electrons in its outermost shell. 1
• Dative covalent bond formed between the O atom in  \( H_{2}O \)  and  \( H^{+} \)  by sharing electron pair. 1

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The diagram below shows a set-up for the electrolysis of a colourless solution of 1 M  \( K_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  containing phenolphthalein indicator.

[IMG:q4_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: State, with explanation, the expected observation around the following electrodes during the electrolysis :

children.label: i
children.question_text: electrode A
children.answer: A: OH⁻(aq) ions are preferentially discharged to give a colourless gas oxygen.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: electrode B
children.answer:
B:
• H⁺(aq) ions are preferentially discharged to give a colourless gas hydrogen. 
• The solution turns pink as [OH⁻(aq)] > [H⁺(aq)] when H⁺(aq) ions are consumed.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Write the equation of the overall reaction in the electrolysis.
sub_questions.answer: 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Explain whether there are any changes in the expected observation around the following electrodes during the electrolysis if the 1 M  \( K_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  is replaced with 1 M  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \) :

children.label: i
children.question_text: electrode A
children.answer: A: No change. OH⁻(aq) ions are still preferentially discharged to give a colourless gas oxygen.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: electrode B
children.answer:
B:
• No change. H⁺(aq) ions, the only cations, are discharged to give a colourless gas hydrogen. 
• No colour change in the solution / The solution will not turn into pink as it is still acidic, despite the decrease in [H⁺(aq)] / as H⁺(aq) is in excess.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Explain the following increasing order of the boiling points of three substances :
\(\mathrm{H}_{2}<\mathrm{F}_{2}<\mathrm{HF} \)
questions.answer:
• Both molecules of H₂ and F₂ are held by weak van der Waals' forces. 1
• The van der Waals' forces between F₂ are stronger than those between H₂ because larger size of F₂ than H₂. 1
• Hydrogen bond exists among HF molecules and hydrogen bond is stronger than van der Waals' forces. 1

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: Concentrated sulphuric acid is a reagent commonly found in laboratories.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text:
Circle TWO hazard warning labels that should be displayed on a bottle of concentrated sulphuric acid :

[IMG:q6_img_0.png]
sub_questions.answer: oxidising and corrosive

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: In order to determine the concentration of a sample of concentrated sulphuric acid,  \( 5.00 \, cm^{3} \)  of the sample was diluted to  \( 1000.0 \, cm^{3} \)  with deionised water. Portions of  \( 25.00 \, cm^{3} \)  of the diluted sample were titrated with  \( 0.189 \, mol \, dm^{-3} \, NaOH(aq) \)  using methyl orange as an indicator. An average of  \( 22.20 \, cm^{3} \)  of NaOH(aq) was used to reach the end point.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Explain why concentrated sulphuric acid should NOT be titrated directly with NaOH(aq).
children.answer: The reaction between concentrated sulphuric acid and NaOH(aq) is highly exothermic.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State the colour change at the end point of the titration.
children.answer: red to orange

children.label: iii
children.question_text: Calculate the concentration of the sample of concentrated sulphuric acid, in \(mol\ dm^{-3}\).
children.answer:
Number of moles of NaOH(aq) used = 0.189 × 22.20 × 10⁻³ = 4.20 × 10⁻³ 
Concentration of the concentrated sulphuric acid 
= 4.20 × 10⁻³ ÷ (2 × 25 × 10⁻³) × (1000 ÷ 5) 
= 16.8 mol dm⁻³

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: With the help of a chemical equation, state the observation when hot concentrated sulphuric acid reacts with copper.
sub_questions.answer:
Copper dissolves. / The solution turns blue. / A colourless / choking gas evolves. 
Cu + 2H₂SO₄ → CuSO₄ + 2H₂O + SO₂

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Ethyne is a gaseous hydrocarbon with molecular formula  \( C_{2}H_{2} \) .

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why the enthalpy change of formation of  \( C_{2}H_{2}(g) \)  CANNOT be determined directly by experiment.
sub_questions.answer: The reaction between carbon and hydrogen does not only give ethyne.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Hess's law can be used to find enthalpy changes which CANNOT be determined directly by experiment. State Hess's law.
sub_questions.answer: The total enthalpy change of a chemical reaction is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Based on the enthalpy changes of combustion  \( \Delta H_{c} \)  of  \( C_{2}H_{2}(g) \) , C(graphite) and  \( H_{2}(g) \)  to construct an enthalpy change cycle and applying Hess's law can give the enthalpy change of formation of  \( C_{2}H_{2}(g) \) .

children.label: i
children.question_text: Draw, with labels, this enthalpy change cycle.
children.answer: [IMG:ans_q7_c_0.jpg]

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
The standard enthalpy changes of combustion \(\Delta H^{\theta}_{c} \) of \(C_2H_2(g)\), C(graphite) and \(H_2(g)\) are given below:
\(\Delta H^{\theta}_{c}\ /\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \)
\(C_2H_2(g)\)-1300
C(graphite)-394
\(H_2(g)\)-286

children.label: a
children.question_text: State the standard conditions for 'standard enthalpy change'.
children.answer: 298K and 1 atm

children.label: b
children.question_text: Calculate the standard enthalpy change of formation of \(C_2H_2(g)\).
children.answer:
Standard enthalpy change of formation of \(C_2H_2(g) = 2 \times (-394) +(-286)-(-1300)\)
\( = +226\ kJ\ mol^{-1}\)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Combustion of petrol increases the concentration of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, and may contribute to global warming. Combustion of petrol also emits poisonous air pollutants.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Write a chemical equation for the complete combustion of octane  \( \left(\mathrm{C}_{8}\mathrm{H}_{18}\right) \) , a component in petrol.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Draw the electron diagram for a molecule of carbon dioxide, showing electrons in the outermost shells only.
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q8_a_1.jpg]

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
Give one reason FOR and one reason AGAINST the following statement :

'Switching from using petrol-driven cars to using electric cars can help alleviate global warming.'

FOR :

AGAINST :
sub_questions.answer:
• 'For': Using carbon capture techniques, the  \( CO_{2} \)  produced in power stations can be trapped, thus the emission of  \( CO_{2} \)  into the atmosphere will be reduced.
• 'Against': The electricity used in powering cars is mainly produced by burning of fossil fuels, and the  \( CO_{2} \)  so produced will still be emitted into the atmosphere.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: Carbon monoxide is one of the poisonous air pollutants emitted from the combustion of petrol. Under what condition would carbon monoxide be formed during the combustion of petrol?
sub_questions.answer: limited supply of air or oxygen

children.label: i
children.question_text: Name a device that can be installed in petrol-driven cars so as to reduce the emission of carbon monoxide.
children.answer: catalytic converter

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Suggest one air pollutant in car exhaust which cannot be removed by the device in (i).
children.answer: particulates / suspended particulates / sulphur dioxide

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Four unlabelled reagent bottles each contains one of the colourless liquids listed below :

\( \mathrm{HOCH_{2}CH_{2}CH_{2}OH}\quad\mathrm{CH_{3}CO_{2}CH_{3}}\quad\mathrm{CH_{3}CH_{2}CO_{2}
H}\quad\mathrm{CH_{2}=CHCO_{2}H} \)
Suggest chemical tests to distinguish the four liquids.
questions.answer:
Chemical knowledge
• Acidified  \( K_{2}Cr_{2}O_{7}(aq) \)  test: Only  \( HOCH_{2}CH_{2}CH_{2}OH \)  will produce a colour change from orange to green.
•  \( Br_{2} \) (in organic solvent) test: Only  \( CH_{2}=CHCO_{2}H \)  will produce a colour change from brown / orange to colourless.
• Add each liquid into water, and then perform Mg / Zn test: Only  \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CO_{2}H \)  or  \( CH_{2}=CHCO_{2}H \)  reacts to give a colourless gas hydrogen.
•  \( CH_{3}CO_{2}CH_{3} \)  gives negative results in all the above three chemical tests.
Communication mark

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In an experiment performed under room conditions as shown below,  \( 5.00 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( \mathrm{H_{2}O_{2}(aq)} \)  decomposed into  \( \mathrm{O_{2}(g)} \)  and  \( \mathrm{H_{2}O(l)} \)  in the presence of a catalyst.  \( \mathrm{O_{2}(g)} \)  was continuously released from the start of the experiment until the third minute when a total of  \( 60 \, cm^{3} \)  of gas was collected. After that, no more gas was collected.

[IMG:q10_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text:
Calculate the initial concentration of the  \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \) , in mol dm\( ^{-3} \) . 
(Molar volume of gas at room conditions = 24 dm\( ^{3} \) )

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
In the graph below, sketch the variation of the volume of gas collected with time in the first 4 minutes.

[IMG:q10_img_1.png]
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q10_a_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: The experiment is repeated using a  \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \)  at a higher temperature but other conditions remain unchanged. Explain whether the total volume of gas obtained would still be  \( 60 \, cm^{3} \) . (The volume of gas is measured at room conditions.)
sub_questions.answer: \( 60 \, cm^{3} \)  of gas would still be collected because the number of moles of  \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \)  is the same for both experiments.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest another method that can be used to follow the progress of this reaction.
sub_questions.answer: Follow the change in total pressure / mass in the system.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The equation below shows the ionisation of 4-nitrophenol in water :

[IMG:q11_img_0.png]

At  \( 25^{\circ} \) C, the equilibrium constant  \( K_{e} \)  for the ionisation is  \( 8.0 \times 10^{-8} \)  mol dm \( ^{-3} \) .

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text:
Write an expression for  \( K_{e} \) .
(You may use HA to represent 4-nitrophenol and  \( A^{-} \)  to represent 4-nitrophenoxide ion.)
sub_questions.answer:
\( K_{c} = [H^{+}(aq)][A^{-}(aq)] / [HA(aq)] \)
where HA represents 4-nitrophenol and  \( A^{-} \) represents 4-nitrophenoxide ion.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: When the above ionisation attains equilibrium at  \( 25^{\circ} \) C, the pH of an aqueous solution of 4-nitrophenol is 2.4. Calculate the ratio of the concentration of 4-nitrophenol to the concentration of 4-nitrophenoxide ions in this solution.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest if there is any colour change when NaOH(aq) is added gradually into the solution in (b). Explain your answer.
sub_questions.answer:
• The equilibrium position will shift to right when  \( H^{+}(aq) \)  ions are consumed by NaOH(aq).
• HA is colourless while A⁻ is yellow. Increase in [A⁻] causes the solution changes from colourless to yellow (or the colour / yellow colour becomes more intense).

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest one possible use of 4-nitrophenol in acid-base titration experiments.
sub_questions.answer: indicator

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Write the structural formula of C.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Deduce the structural formula of B.
children.answer:
• The -OH group in B after reacting with HBr will change to the -Br group in C, and there is no chiral carbon due to no optical activity.
• Thus the structure of B is  \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CH(OH)CH_{2}CH_{3} \) .

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Name the type of reaction for the conversion of B to C.
children.answer: Substitution

children.label: i
children.question_text: Deduce the structural formula of A. Label on this structural formula all chiral centre(s), if any, by using ‘*’.
children.answer:
A has a C=C double bond as there are 2 hydrogen atoms less in A as compared with B. Moreover, A is optically active and it has a chiral carbon.
A has the structure  \( CH_{2}=C(*)HCH(OH)CH_{2}CH_{3} \)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State the reagent(s) required for the conversion of A to B.
children.answer: \( H_{2}/Pd \)  (heat) or  \( H_{2}/Pt \)  (heat) or  \( H_{2}/Ni \)  (heat)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Outline a synthetic route, with no more than three steps, to accomplish the following conversion. For each step, give the reagent(s), reaction conditions (as appropriate) and the structure of the organic product.

[IMG:q13_img_0.png]

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
At  \( 60^{\circ} \) C,  \( \mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \)  reacts with  \( \mathrm{C}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) \)  in an acidic medium to give  \( \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \) ,  \( \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{g}) \)  and  \( \mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \) . The graph below shows the variation of the colour intensity of the reaction mixture with time.

[IMG:q14_img_0.png]

Based on the information above, write the chemical equation for the reaction and illustrate THREE characteristics of transition metals exhibited by manganese.
questions.answer:
Chemical knowledge
•  \( 2MnO_{4}^{-}(aq) + 5C_{2}O_{4}^{2-}(aq) + 16H^{+}(aq) \rightarrow 2Mn^{2+}(aq) + 10CO_{2}(g) + 8H_{2}O(l) \)
• Manganese exhibits variable oxidation numbers. The oxidation number of manganese changes from +7 in  \( MnO_{4}^{-}(aq) \)  ions to +2 in  \( Mn^{2+}(aq) \)  ions in the reaction.
• Manganese forms coloured ions in aqueous solutions.  \( MnO_{4}^{-}(aq) \)  ions exhibit purple /  \( Mn^{2+}(aq) \)  ions exhibit pale pink.
• From the graph, it shows that the reaction rate increases when  \( Mn^{2+}(aq) \)  ions form / when the reaction proceeds.
• It shows that  \( Mn^{2+}(aq) \)  ions act as a catalyst for the reaction.
Communication mark
